title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
short_title: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law stops the government from using taxpayer money to pay for abortions or health insurance that covers abortions. The only times this rule does not apply is when the pregnancy happened because of rape or incest, or when the mother's life is in danger. The law also says that government hospitals and government doctors cannot perform abortions except in those same special cases. Health insurance companies must tell people clearly if their plans cover abortions and how much extra it costs.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for co

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law can be called the No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion and Abortion Insurance Full Disclosure Act of 2025. The law has two main parts. The first part prohibits using taxpayer money to pay for abortions. The second part explains how this applies to health insurance under the Affordable Care Act.

sections.title: Section 101 Prohibiting Taxpayer Funded Abortions
sections.text: This section adds a new chapter to the United States Code that creates rules about using government money for abortions. No money that comes from federal taxes or federal programs can be spent on abortions. No federal money can be spent on health insurance plans that cover abortions. Federal hospitals and doctors who work for the government cannot perform abortions as part of their job. People and states can still buy separate abortion coverage with their own private money, but they cannot use any federal matching funds. This law does not change other existing federal laws about abortion funding. Federal money can be used to treat medical problems that happen because of an abortion. There are exceptions that allow federal money to be used for abortions when the pregnancy resulted from rape or incest, or when the mother's life is in danger. These rules also apply to Washington DC.

sections.title: Section 102 Amendment to Table of Chapters
sections.text: This section updates the official list of chapters in the United States Code to include the new chapter about prohibiting taxpayer funded abortions.

sections.title: Section 201 Clarifying Application of Prohibition to Premium Credits and Cost-Sharing Reductions Under ACA
sections.text: This section changes tax rules in the Affordable Care Act. People cannot get tax credits or cost-sharing help from the government to pay for health insurance plans that cover abortions, except for cases involving rape, incest, or when the mother's life is in danger. People can still buy separate abortion coverage with their own money, but they cannot get government help to pay for it. Small businesses also cannot get tax credits for health insurance plans that cover abortions. Insurance companies can still offer separate abortion coverage, but employers cannot use tax credits to help pay for it. These changes apply to health plans that start after December 31, 2025. The rules also apply to multi-state health plans offered through government exchanges, which cannot provide abortion coverage that would be paid for with federal money.

sections.title: Section 202 Revision of Notice Requirements Regarding Disclosure of Extent of Health Plan Coverage of Abortion and Abortion Premium Surcharges
sections.text: This section requires health insurance plans to clearly tell customers whether they cover abortion services. This information must be shown when people sign up for insurance and must be easy to see in all advertising materials, comparison tools, and benefit summaries. If a health plan covers abortions and charges an extra fee for that coverage, that extra charge must be listed separately and clearly identified in all materials where the premium cost is shown. These new disclosure rules start thirty days after this law is passed.
